stimulus: To examine how room lighting affects test performance, British researchers had students complete identical math problems under different conditions. The researchers found that when the lighting was warm-toned and dim, students tended to make more arithmetic errors. ______blank cool-toned and bright lighting was associated with fewer errors.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "By contrast" logically signals that the finding described in this sentence contrasts with the finding described in the previous sentence. That is, cool/bright lighting was associated with fewer errors, whereas warm/dim lighting was associated with more errors.

Choice B is incorrect because "specifically" illogically signals that the sentence provides precise details about the earlier finding; instead, it presents a contrasting finding. Choice C is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals exemplification, when the sentence presents a contrasting result. Choice D is incorrect because "firstly" illogically signals a first item in a series.
